vocab.meaning: một kho tàng kiến thức/thông tin
vocab.example: My archaeology professor is a real mine of information, making every lecture feel like a discovery.

vocab.meaning: thấu hiểu một vấn đề phức tạp
vocab.example: It took me a while to get my head around macroeconomics, but now it’s the most captivating part of my degree.

vocab.meaning: mở ra những cơ hội mới
vocab.example: Studying international relations has opened new doors for me to understand global politics from a different perspective.

vocab.meaning: thách thức khi phải học nhiều thứ mới trong thời gian ngắn
vocab.example: There was a steep learning curve when I started coding, but the thrill of building my first app was unbeatable.
The seminars on environmental ethics always provide plenty of food for thought regarding our impact on the planet.

vocab.meaning: mở rộng tầm hiểu biết/nhãn quan
vocab.example: Learning about diverse cultures in my sociology class has truly helped broaden my horizons.
In a nutshell, the most interesting thing is seeing how abstract theories apply to real-world scenarios.

vocab.meaning: giao thoa điểm tốt của cả hai bên
vocab.example: My major combines art and technology, giving me the best of both worlds in terms of creativity and logic.

vocab.meaning: phần nổi của tảng băng chìm (vẫn còn rất nhiều điều để khám phá)
vocab.example: What I've learned about psychology so far is just the tip of the iceberg; there is so much more to uncover.

vocab.meaning: tạo ra lợi thế cạnh tranh cho bản thân
vocab.example: Mastering data analysis during my studies will definitely give me an edge when I enter the job market.
